payload.name: capture
payload.description: Capture a task onto the board. Join argv into one task text and emit task.captured; new tasks start in the Now lane. The board's capture box and the brain both call this.

params.text: string — the task, joined from argv

payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# capture — drop anything onto the board. Joins argv into one task and emits a
# single task.captured event; new tasks start in the "now" lane. Pipe contract:
# args via argv, log as JSONL on stdin (unused), new events as JSONL on stdout.
import sys, json
text = " ".join(sys.argv[1:]).strip()
if not text:
    text = "(empty task)"
print(json.dumps({"name": "task.captured", "payload": {"text": text}}))

payload.name: move
payload.description: Move a task to a lane. Positional argv: task id (the seq of its task.captured event) then lane (now | this_week | waiting | done; friendly spellings normalized). Emits task.moved. The board's per-card buttons and the brain both call this.
payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# move — send a task to a lane. argv is positional: the task's id (the seq of its
# task.captured event) then the lane (now | this_week | waiting | done). Friendly
# spellings are normalized. Emits one task.moved event.
import sys, json
args = sys.argv[1:]
task = args[0] if args else ""
lane = " ".join(args[1:]).strip().lower() or "now"
ALIAS = {"this week": "this_week", "thisweek": "this_week", "todo": "now",
         "inbox": "now", "today": "now", "complete": "done", "completed": "done",
         "finished": "done", "blocked": "waiting", "later": "waiting"}
lane = ALIAS.get(lane, lane)
if lane not in ("now", "this_week", "waiting", "done"):
    lane = "now"
try:
    task = int(task)
except (ValueError, TypeError):
    pass
print(json.dumps({"name": "task.moved", "payload": {"task": task, "lane": lane}}))

payload.name: board
payload.description: A Now / This week / Waiting / Done board. Folds task.captured (task id = its seq, starts in Now) and task.moved (id, lane) into lanes, rendering each task as a card with one-click move forms (posting to /run/move) and a capture box at top (posting to /run/capture). Bare semantic HTML, kernel class vocabulary.

examples.note: shows the lanes and places a moved task under its lane

payload.name: board

payload.text: Capture your first real task in the box above ↑
Try the buttons on this card to move me → Done

payload.name: plan
payload.description: Set the meal for a day. Positional argv: day (mon..sun; friendly spellings like 'monday' normalized) then the meal text. Re-planning a day overwrites it. Emits meal.planned. The kitchen board and the brain both call this.

params.meal: string — the meal, joined from the rest

payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# plan — set the meal for a day. Positional argv: day (mon..sun, friendly
# spellings ok) then the meal (joined). Re-planning a day overwrites it. Emits
# meal.planned. Pipe contract: argv in, log on stdin (unused), events on stdout.
import sys, json
args = sys.argv[1:]
day = (args[0].lower() if args else "")
ALIAS = {"monday":"mon","tuesday":"tue","wednesday":"wed","thursday":"thu",
         "friday":"fri","saturday":"sat","sunday":"sun"}
day = ALIAS.get(day, day)
meal = " ".join(args[1:]).strip() or "(no meal)"
print(json.dumps({"name": "meal.planned", "payload": {"day": day, "meal": meal}}))

payload.name: shop
payload.description: Add an item to the shopping/prep list. Joins argv into one item. Emits shopping.added; the item's id is this event's seq. The kitchen board and the brain both call this.

params.item: string — the item, joined from argv

payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# shop — add an item to the shopping list. Joins argv into one item. Emits
# shopping.added; the item's id is the seq the kernel assigns to this event.
import sys, json
item = " ".join(sys.argv[1:]).strip() or "(item)"
print(json.dumps({"name": "shopping.added", "payload": {"item": item}}))

payload.name: got
payload.description: Check a shopping item off. argv[0] is the item's id (the seq of its shopping.added event). Emits shopping.bought.
payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# got — check a shopping item off. argv[0] is the item's id (the seq of its
# shopping.added event). Emits shopping.bought.
import sys, json
args = sys.argv[1:]
t = args[0] if args else ""
try:
    t = int(t)
except (ValueError, TypeError):
    pass
print(json.dumps({"name": "shopping.bought", "payload": {"item": t}}))

examples.note: marks an item bought by id

payload.name: kitchen
payload.description: Meal plan + shopping/prep. Folds meal.planned (day, meal; latest per day wins) into a Mon..Sun week, and shopping.added (item, id=seq) / shopping.bought (item id) into a shopping list (to-get + got). A capture form per day posts to /run/plan; the list has add (/run/shop) and 'got it' (/run/got) forms. Bare semantic HTML, kernel class vocabulary.
payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3 # kitchen — meal plan + shopping/prep, replayed from the log. Folds meal.planned # (day, meal; latest per day wins), shopping.added (item, id = its seq) and # shopping.bought (item id) into a weekly plan and a shopping list. Bare semantic # HTML using the kernel class vocabulary; the kernel themes it. import sys, json from html import escape DAYS = [("mon","Mon"),("tue","Tue"),("wed","Wed"),("thu","Thu"), ("fri","Fri"),("sat","Sat"),("sun","Sun")] week, shop, order, bought = {}, {}, [], set() for line in sys.stdin: line = line.strip() if not line: continue try: e = json.loads(line) except Exception: continue n = e.get("name"); p = e.get("payload", {}) or {} if n == "meal.planned": week[p.get("day","")] = p.get("meal","") elif n == "shopping.added": sid = e.get("seq"); shop[sid] = p.get("item",""); order.append(sid) elif n == "shopping.bought": it = p.get("item") try: it = int(it) except (ValueError, TypeError): pass bought.add(it) print("") print("kitchen") print("

kitchen

") print("

This week

") print("") for key, label in DAYS: meal = week.get(key, "") cell = escape(meal) if meal else "—" print("" % (escape(label), cell, key)) print("
daymeal
%s%s" "
" "" "" "
") to_buy = [(sid, shop[sid]) for sid in order if sid not in bought] got = [(sid, shop[sid]) for sid in order if sid in bought] print("

Shopping list (%d to get)

" % len(to_buy)) print("
" "
") if not to_buy: print("

nothing to buy

") for sid, item in to_buy: print("
%s" "
" "" "
" % (escape(item), sid)) if got: print("

got

") for sid, item in got: print("
✓ %s
" % escape(item)) print("")

examples.note: renders the week and the shopping list

#!/usr/bin/env python3
# plan — set the meal for a day. Positional argv: day (mon..sun, friendly
# spellings ok) then the meal (joined). Re-planning a day overwrites it. Emits
# meal.planned. Pipe contract: argv in, log on stdin (unused), events on stdout.
import sys, json
args = sys.argv[1:]
day = (args[0].lower() if args else "")
ALIAS = {"monday":"mon","tuesday":"tue","wednesday":"wed","thursday":"thu",
         "friday":"fri","saturday":"sat","sunday":"sun"}
day = ALIAS.get(day, day)
meal = " ".join(args[1:]).strip() or "(no meal)"
print(json.dumps({"name": "meal.planned", "payload": {"day": day, "meal": meal}}))
